title: Sleeper bus quality varies wildly — research the specific operator
note: The difference between a good and bad sleeper bus is enormous. Loud all-night stops, poorly maintained bunks, and unscheduled detours are common on budget operators. Futa Bus and Phuong Trang have better track records.

title: Vietnam's climate runs north to south on a delay
note: When the north is cool and dry (Oct–Dec), central Vietnam may be flooded, and the south is warm and sunny. Check weather for your specific cities — not 'Vietnam' as a whole. The difference can be 25°C and entirely opposite conditions.

title: City traffic is a skill — you learn it in one morning
note: Ho Chi Minh City traffic is genuinely intense but follows logic. Walk steadily into the flow and bikes route around you. Stopping suddenly or rushing is what causes near-misses. One slow morning crossing is enough to get the rhythm.

title: Always agree on a price before getting in an unlicensed taxi
note: Grab is the reliable option in cities. For xe ôm or unmarked cabs, settle the fare before departure — the post-ride ask is often 3–5× the reasonable fare even when you showed them a map. No negotiating after.

title: Install your VPN before landing
note: Some services and platforms face intermittent access issues in Vietnam depending on the provider and the day. Setting up a VPN on a local SIM after arrival is more complicated than doing it at home on your regular connection.
